title: http.rs: Content-Length check uses 'as usize' which truncates on 32-bit
description:
LOCATION: src/http.rs:8, read_body_limited()

FINDING: The Content-Length fast-reject at line 8 casts 'len as usize'. On a 32-bit target, usize is 32 bits. If Content-Length is e.g. 5_000_000_000 (5GB), 'len as usize' truncates to 705_032_704 on a 32-bit platform, which is less than many max_bytes values, bypassing the fast-reject entirely. The streaming limit at line 15 would still catch it, but the Content-Length advisory check provides misleading behavior.

While broadside targets 64-bit Linux (musl Docker), the code has no compile-time assertion preventing misuse on 32-bit, and the truncation is silent.

IMPACT: On 32-bit targets, a server advertising a fraudulent large Content-Length header bypasses the fast-reject, falling through to the streaming limit. Minor — streaming limit still protects — but the guard is silently broken.
close_reason: Fixed: Content-Length compared as u64 not cast to usize
